domain: Norse bind-rune (Migration-era Germanic ligature): 2-3 adjacent runes fused into single glyph sharing common vertical stave; same-stave variant shares stem-line; reading requires alphabet + ligature knowledge; preserved decomposability.
specific_mechanism: Fusion of 2-3 atomic symbols into compound glyph with preserved decomposability; the compound occupies one slot but meaning is conjunction of constituents.
llm_application: BIND-RUNE 2-token ligature conjunction merge: augment BPE/Tiktoken tokenizer with FIXED PRE-ENCODE merge table of HIGH-MI token pairs (top-N=4096 from calibration). At encode time, every (t1,t2) ∈ MergeTable → T_bind(t1,t2) (reserved id). At decode time, T_bind splits to (t1,t2) before downstream logic. T_bind embedding = (t1_emb + t2_emb)/2. Sequence length reduced 10-20% for frequent bigrams; NO LLM RETRAIN. Differs from (a) standard BPE (learn merges during pretrain), (b) SuperBPE (cross-word superwords during training), (c) LiteToken (removes residues during training), (d) BoundlessBPE (cross-boundary during training), (e) Multi-Token Prediction (decoder side) by combining (i) FIXED-PAIR HIGH-MI merge table + (ii) POST-HOC tokenizer augmentation + (iii) DECODE-TIME split.
novelty_claim: BPE learns merges during training. SuperBPE cross-word superwords. LiteToken removes residues. BoundlessBPE cross-boundary. BIND-RUNE recombines (a) FIXED-PAIR table + (b) POST-HOC no-retrain + (c) DECODE-TIME split. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
